title: Blood on Cashmere
timeEstimate: Pro-lane immediately; home is cold blot, no chemistry
stainChemistry: Cashmere is the finest protein fiber in common use — much thinner and more delicate than wool. Everything that goes wrong with wool happens faster on cashmere: felts on lighter friction, weakens with shorter chemistry dwell. Blood adds a second protein on top of the cashmere protein, and enzyme cleaners that lift blood from cotton would damage cashmere too. Hot water sets the blood AND damages the cashmere in seconds. The home failure on blood-cashmere isn't "the stain didn't come out" — it's "the cashmere felted and now it's permanently ruined." For high-value cashmere, the honest answer is no home chemistry — straight to a delicates specialist.
whyThisWorks: Cold-water blot extracts bulk blood before it sets. A cashmere specialist can then use ice water plus a gentle wool-safe detergent plus strict-timed acid spotter plus extreme-dilution peroxide (or reducing bleach for oxidized blood) to clear what's left without felting. Home tools work for one thing only: extracting bulk before transit. Anything else is felt-risk territory on cashmere.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): cashmere fibers are 14–15 microns vs wool at 20–30 microns; finer keratin scales open at lower alkaline thresholds and felt under lighter friction. Pro uses ice-cold water + baby-shampoo-grade NSD + timed acetic acid + extreme-dilution peroxide; sodium hydrosulfite at controlled dilution for oxidized blood.
defaultAssumption: Cashmere + blood = pro IMMEDIATELY. For high-value garments (most cashmere), home protocol is zero chemistry, just cold-water blot in transit to the cleaner. Repeat home rounds on cashmere felt the fiber every time.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Ice water spray, white cloth, gloves
spottingProtocol.instruction: Gloves on (biohazard). Vertical press cold water onto the BACK with a white cloth. Pressure ONLY, never agitation — cashmere felts on lighter friction than wool. Replace cloth as it picks up red.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Baby shampoo (Cetaphil / Johnson's) at 2% in cold water, sponge
spottingProtocol.instruction: Baby shampoo is pH-neutral — gentlest household-grade NSD. Sponge press only, never rub. Suspends blood without alkalinity.
Multiple cold rinses until water runs clear. Cashmere holds residue tightly — thorough rinsing essential. Press, never squeeze.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Acetic Acid (diluted 1:20 — more dilute than wool)
spottingProtocol.instruction: More dilute than wool (1:20 not 1:10). Dropper, press cloth, 60-sec ceiling. Cashmere weakens faster than wool with acid.
More dilute than wool (1:2). Sponge press, strict 1–2 min, monitor. Cashmere yellows faster than wool with peroxide.
Multiple rinses, residue sets in cashmere fast. Press between dry towels. Lay flat to air-dry, reshape damp.

steps: 1. Gloves. Cold press-blot at first contact, no agitation.
2. Baby shampoo NSD sponge press, 2% in cold water.
5. H₂O₂ 1:2 dilute for oxidized residue, 1–2 min strict.
6. Final cold rinse, press dry, lay flat.
products: Baby shampoo (Cetaphil / Johnson's) — household-grade cashmere NSD
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — cashmere felts even faster than wool
warnings: NEVER hot water — felts cashmere in seconds + sets blood
NEVER agitation / rubbing — felts cashmere on contact when wet
NEVER alkaline / OxiClean — opens scales irreversibly
Strict 60-sec acid + 1–2 min peroxide — both more dilute than wool

homeSolutions.agent: Honest Answer — Pro Immediately for High-Value Cashmere
homeSolutions.instruction: Cashmere is the most delicate common fiber. The wool felting risk is amplified — friction that wouldn't felt regular wool DOES felt cashmere. Home rounds on cashmere felt the fiber more often than they lift the stain. For any meaningful cashmere garment (most cashmere), the right answer is to apply NO chemistry at home and take it straight to a delicates specialist. Time-wise, your home job is cold-water blot to prevent blood from setting in transit, and that's it.

homeSolutions.agent: Gloves + Cold Water Press-Blot — Pressure Only
homeSolutions.instruction: Disposable gloves on (biohazard). Place a white towel under the stain. Press a SECOND white cloth dampened in COLD/ice water onto the BACK of the stain. PRESSURE ONLY — no rubbing, no scrubbing, no wiping motion. Cashmere felts on lighter friction than wool. Lift the cloth, refresh, press again. 60–90 seconds of patient pressing usually pulls fresh blood to a pale shadow.

homeSolutions.agent: Press Dry — DO NOT Apply Anything Else
homeSolutions.instruction: Press the cashmere between two dry white towels to lift moisture. No wringing, no twisting, no hanging wet. Lay flat on a fresh towel at room temperature, out of sun. Do NOT apply baby shampoo, vinegar, peroxide, or anything else at home. Even gentle chemistry is an unnecessary felt risk. The garment's best outcome is reaching the cleaner with nothing applied.

Don't wait — cashmere has the narrowest treatment window. Call ahead: "Do you handle cashmere?" Many cleaners say no to cashmere. When you bring it in, tell them: "Blood on cashmere, age [hours], cold-water press-blot ONLY at home. No detergent, no chemistry applied. [Color: still red / drifting brown]." Pro uses cold NSD + 1:20 acetic acid + extreme-dilute peroxide with strict timing — chemistry tuned for cashmere's friction sensitivity that home cannot replicate.

steps: 1. Cashmere + blood = pro IMMEDIATELY for any high-value garment.
2. Gloves on. Cold-water press-blot from the BACK, pressure only.
3. Press dry between white towels — no other chemistry.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop after cold-water press-blot. Any chemistry at home is felt-risk territory.
warnings: NEVER hot water — felts cashmere in seconds
NEVER agitation / friction — felts cashmere on lighter touch than wool
PRESSURE only on every step, no wiping motion

fiberSensitivities: Cashmere keratin felts faster than wool
Yellows faster than wool with peroxide
Weakens faster than wool with acid

materialWarnings: Pro IMMEDIATELY for any meaningful cashmere garment
Cold press-blot is the entire home protocol
Friction = felt — cashmere felts on lighter friction than wool

escalation.when: Always — cashmere + blood is pro-lane immediately. The garment's best outcome is reaching the specialist with no home chemistry applied.
escalation.whatToTell: "Blood on cashmere, age [hours / overnight], cold-water press-blot ONLY at home. No chemistry applied. [Color: still red / drifting brown]."
escalation.specialistType: delicates / cashmere specialist (call ahead — many cleaners decline cashmere; confirm baby-shampoo NSD + reducing bleach capability)

contextQuestions: How long ago — minutes, hours, or older?
Color — still bright red, or drifting brown (oxidized)?
Cashmere weight — fine knit, heavier knit, woven?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Fine-gauge cashmere knits felt faster than heavier knits. Oxidized brown blood is much harder — needs reducing bleach at controlled dilution, pro-only on cashmere. Pre-applied enzyme cleaner = cashmere may already be partially digested; pre-applied hot water = may already be partially felted. Disclose honestly.
